meta.title: The Future is No Longer a Prediction. It is an AI-Generated Graph
meta.description:
The Topologization of Semantic Flow: Discretizing Associative Language into Structural AI Reasoning Graphs.

Deconstructing 5 ArXiv pre-prints on the Topologization of Language, Reasoning, and our Collective Future.

The Final Insight: AI isn't just predicting what will happen next. Through PseudoAct's code, RUMAD's swarms, and the Corporate Imaginary's rhetoric, AI is literally compiling the future.

The future is no longer a prediction; it is an AI generated graph.

All rights w/ authors: 

1st Pre-print
RUMAD: Reinforcement-Unifying Multi-Agent Debate
AAAI Track
Chao Wang
Tsinghua University
Shenzhen, China
wangchao23@mails.tsinghua.edu.cn
Han Lin
Zhejiang University
Hangzhou, China
Tsinghua University
Shenzhen, China
hlin@zju.edu.cn
Huaze Tang
Tsinghua University
Shenzhen, China
thz21@mails.tsinghua.edu.cn
Huijing Lin
Tsinghua University
Shenzhen, China
linhj24@mails.tsinghua.edu.cn
Wenbo Ding
Tsinghua University
Shenzhen, China
ding.wenbo@sz.tsinghua.edu.cn
arXiv:2602.23864


2nd Pre-print
HIERARCHICAL CONCEPT-BASED INTERPRETABLE MODELS
Oscar Hill
University of Cambridge
ogh22@cam.ac.uk
Mateo Espinosa Zarlenga
University of Oxford
trin4380@ox.ac.uk
Mateja Jamnik
University of Cambridge
mj201@cam.ac.uk
arXiv:2602.23947


3rd Pre-print
LLM-Driven Multi-Turn Task-Oriented Dialogue Synthesis for Realistic
Reasoning
Yu Zhu, Kai Yang
Department of Computer Science and Technology, Tongji University
arXiv:2602.2361


4th Pre-print
PseudoAct: Leveraging Pseudocode Synthesis for Flexible Planning and Action Control in Large
Language Model Agents
Yihan (Logon) Wen, Xin Chen
Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering, Texas A&M University, College Station, TX, USA
arXiv:2602.23668


5th Pre-print
The Compulsory Imaginary: AGI and Corporate Authority
Emilio Barkett
Columbia University
Correspondence: eab2291@columbia.ed
arXiv:2602.23679
